_comment: 손번역 영어 프로즈 오버레이(별명·설명). scripts/build-animal-en.mjs가 기계 생성분 위에 덮어씀. 없는 종은 우아하게 생략(en/sci·수치로 degrade).

0001.desc: Prairie dogs are adorable animals that build huge towns underground. They warn each other of danger with barking calls and live together with family and friends. They are great jumpers, too — like tiny superheroes!

0002.desc: The otter is a water-dwelling animal that looks like a weasel. It hunts fish and frogs and digs dens along riverbanks or under rocks. Otters usually hunt alone, but sometimes catch food together as a family.

0003.desc: The red panda looks like a small reddish bear. It mostly eats bamboo and is active at night. It is endangered and needs our protection.

0004.desc: Meerkats are cute animals with long tails. They live in groups on the grasslands of southern Africa. Timon from Disney's The Lion King is a meerkat!

0005.desc: The capybara is a huge rodent that loves water and swims very well. It mainly eats grasses and fruit and lives in groups with its friends. You can find it in the forests and wetlands of South America.

0006.desc: The alpaca is a lovable animal from South America. Its soft fleece is used to make warm clothes. Alpacas sometimes spit, but mostly they just graze and live in herds.

0007.desc: The arctic fox is a small fox that lives in the Arctic. Its coat turns brown in summer and white or bluish in winter. It hunts small animals such as lemmings and sometimes travels enormous distances.

0008.desc: The hamster is a cute little animal that can store food in its cheek pouches. It is mostly active at night and eats seeds and insects. Hamsters come in many colors and sizes.

0009.desc: The fennec fox is a very small fox that lives in the desert. Its big ears help it stay cool. It searches for food at night and rests during the day.

0010.desc: Zebras live on the African savanna. Their black and white stripes make them easy to recognize. They roam in herds and graze on grass, and kick with their hind legs when a lion appears.

0011.desc: The hippopotamus is a huge animal that lives in rivers and lakes. It spends most of its time in the water and eats plants, though it occasionally eats meat too. Its mouth is so wide that it looks scary when it opens — but hippos also have plenty of fun together in the water.

0012.desc: The rhinoceros is a very large animal with a big horn on its nose. It eats grass and leaves and lives in the forests of Africa and Asia. The horn keeps growing for life, and rhinos are excellent swimmers.

0013.desc: The lion looks like a giant cat. It lives mainly in Africa and India and stays in groups called prides. Lionesses do the hunting while the males guard the pride.

0014.desc: The tiger is a very large member of the cat family. It has orange fur with black stripes and hunts alone in the forest.

0015.desc: The elephant is an enormous animal. It drinks water and pulls down leaves with its long trunk. Elephants travel and live together in family herds.

0016.desc: The giraffe's neck is so long that it can easily reach leaves high up in the trees. It lives mostly in savannas and grasslands and has an enormous body. Every giraffe's coat pattern is different.

0017.desc: The cheetah can run incredibly fast — as fast as a car. Its spotted coat makes it easy to recognize, and it lives mainly on the African grasslands.

0018.desc: The leopard is a big cat covered in black rosettes. It prefers to live alone and hunts mostly at night. It climbs so well that it often hauls its prey up into a tree.

0019.desc: The wolf belongs to the dog family and lives in the forests and fields of North America, Eurasia and North Africa. It travels in packs and mainly hunts large animals. Wolves look like dogs but have bigger bodies and longer legs.

0020.desc: The fox is a small member of the dog family. Its narrow snout and furry ears give it a charming look. Foxes eat all kinds of food and have a habit of burying leftovers.

0021.desc: The polar bear is a large white bear that lives in the Arctic. Its fur is actually see-through and only looks white because of the light. Polar bears mainly hunt seals and swim very well, but global warming is shrinking the place they call home.

0022.desc: The panda is an adorable bear with black and white fur. It mostly eats bamboo and lives in the mountains of China. Pandas like being alone and do not hibernate.

0023.desc: The koala lives in Australia and spends most of its life up in the trees. It eats eucalyptus leaves and sleeps for most of the day. Its cute looks have made it a favorite around the world.

0024.desc: The kangaroo is a large animal that lives in Australia. It mainly eats grass and bounds along on its hind legs. Its tail keeps it balanced, and it can leap a very long way!
